page: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv6 server family. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per vCPU. It features no local storage and no GPU hardware, but is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the D4ls_v6 demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2115.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.28 GB/sec. However, its multi-core capabilities are more limited, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 3908.06 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4761.0, both falling into the lower performance tiers. Memory performance is also a constraint, as evidenced by a weak PassMark memory score of 1647.88 and a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 36.43 GB/sec.

Given its high clock speed but low memory-to-CPU ratio, the D4ls_v6 is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints. It is well-suited for single-threaded processing tasks, text processing, and basic web serving. It is less appropriate for memory-intensive databases, software compilation, or multi-threaded parallel computing due to its memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling limitations.
description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Dlsv6 family, featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance contains no local storage or GPU accelerators but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2115.0 and high single-threaded gzip compression throughput. Conversely, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively weak, as shown by a PassMark memory score of 1647.88. This configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads such as text processing, single-threaded applications, and lightweight web serving, while being less suitable for memory-intensive databases or complex multi-threaded operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, offering strong single-core performance for low-memory workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4ls_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors and a low memory-to-CPU ratio for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
4 dedicated vCPUs with 8.0 GB of system memory
Strong single-core and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for text processing and lightweight web serving
